text: We built something wild. An AI that thinks in full English — but can only talk to you in emoji. Inspired by the show Pantheon, running entirely in your browser.

overlay.title: Thinks in language. Speaks in emoji.
overlay.body: Fine-tuned LFM 2.5 running in-browser via WebGPU. No server needed.

text: Hit start and the model loads straight into your browser. Nine hundred megabytes of consciousness pure client-side magic.
Here's the fun part. Ask it about a happy memory, and watch it think out loud — then light up with emoji. That internal monologue? That's real model output, streaming live.
Offer it coffee and watch what happens. It thinks about what caffeine means when you're digital — then answers with pure warmth. Every emoji is a tiny act of love.

overlay.text: The gap between thought and symbol is the whole experience

text: Beach or mountains? It can't pick either! And it has personality — playful, warm, a little stuck. Every response is this delightful act of creative compression.

Now flip the switch. There's a second model — a decoder — that reads the emoji and reconstructs what was said in plain English. Same idea as Anthropic's natural-language autoencoders, except the latent space is emoji you can actually read.

overlay.text: Decoder loads on demand · emoji → text

text: Watch the round trip. You speak, the encoder squeezes it down to emoji, and the decoder samples three different reconstructions. Same input, three plausible decompressions — you can literally see the lossiness of the channel.

overlay.title: text  →  💔😭🫂💕  →  text
overlay.body: Three reconstructions per message — the lossy bottleneck made visible.

text: Two small models, both running in your browser. An encoder that compresses language into emoji, a decoder that reads it back. The autoencoder you can read. Go try it — both directions are waiting.
